purpose: Corpus-hygiene repetition passes for a staged acquisition manifest, at two grains. Document mode (default) is MinHash-LSH cross-source near-duplicate dedup — it drops near-identical reposts/reprints the exact-hash guard misses. Passage mode (--passages) is the sub-document lens: Stage A clusters near-duplicate raw paragraphs (LSH candidates confirmed by EXACT Jaccard), Stage B is a stdlib exact inverted-index scan that finds every contiguous verbatim span repeated at >= 2 locations regardless of what surrounds it. Passage mode is report-first and emits no verdict; spans are reported for consumer-side masking, never excised.
use_when: You have a staged impostor-corpus manifest and want to remove near-duplicate documents (syndicated reposts, lightly edited reprints, the same essay pulled from two sources) that the exact SHA-256 dedup does not catch.
You are merging acquisition output from multiple sources / directories and need cross-source dedup before the pool feeds voice-distance / General-Imposters baselines.

You need the SUB-document lens (--passages) — a passage repeated inside one document or across otherwise-distinct documents, which both the exact-hash guard and document-level near-dup are structurally blind to. Stage B's detection guarantee is arithmetic rather than thresholded — every verbatim repeated span of at least max(--span-shingle-k, --min-span-words) tokens is reported, exactly and deterministically.

You are staging a corpus for continued pretraining / fine-tuning and want a repeated-span inventory with per-occurrence provenance to drive loss masking or chunk-stream filtering on the training side.
do_not_use_when: You only need exact-duplicate removal — acquisition_core.content_hash_already_present already handles that inline during acquisition.

datasketch is not installed and you cannot add the optional acquisition dep (the pass raises a clear RuntimeError naming requirements-acquisition.txt). Stage B alone (--passages --stages b) runs stdlib-only.

You want to know whether a corpus is PROSE. That is check_corpus.py's question (non-prose contamination — CSS/HTML/code/tables spaCy would POS-tag as prose); this asks whether the prose is REPEATED. A corpus can be 100% clean prose and still carry one passage five times, so the two are orthogonal gates an operator may run on the same staged manifest; neither replaces the other.

The corpus is about to feed a duplicate-dependent set-level-diversity surface (corpus_novelty_audit, homogeneity_audit, distinct_diversity_audit, skeleton_overlap_audit, cross_doc_novelty_profile). Those measure signals that live IN the retained duplicates, so a passage-deduped pool destroys the measured object — they refuse a `passage_dedup`-marked manifest via pool_guard, and the fix is to feed them the PRE-dedup source manifest.
You want an excision / masking tool. Stage B never rewrites documents, and passage mode never rewrites the input manifest.

inputs.target: A JSONL acquisition manifest (inline `text` or a resolvable `text_path`/`path` per row).

artifacts: A deduplicated JSONL manifest (dropped rows removed, all others preserved in order).
A DedupResult report (kept/dropped ids + near-duplicate clusters + threshold provenance), on stdout with --json.

Passage mode: a JSON report (stdout with --json, or --report-out FILE) carrying the Stage-A cluster / Stage-B span inventory with per-occurrence provenance, plus a mandatory `assumptions` params-and-limits block and a real ClaimLicense on the voice_coherence_acquisition surface.

Passage mode with --out MANIFEST --passage-dir DIR: a manifest_validator-clean passage-unit manifest (one row per kept passage, every inheritable source field including ai_status / privacy / consent_status copied verbatim, plus the `passage_dedup` marker) alongside one text file per passage. The export refuses entirely rather than inventing a missing ai_status or use.

compute.cost_note: MinHash-LSH is near-linear in the corpus size; datasketch pulls numpy/scipy. Optional within the acquisition tier — lazy-imported, so base import stays clean. Stage B is pure stdlib but holds an O(corpus tokens) inverted index in one process: fine at staged-personal-manifest scale, shard via shard_runner above it.

references: Deduplicating Training Data Makes Language Models Better (arXiv:2107.06499): https://arxiv.org/abs/2107.06499
Quantifying Memorization Across Neural Language Models (arXiv:2202.07646): https://arxiv.org/abs/2202.07646
